summary: The conjecture of Erdős and Simonovits — that for every finite family $\mathcal{F}$ some member $F \in \mathcal{F}$ satisfies $\mathrm{ex}(n,F) = O(\mathrm{ex}(n,\mathcal{F}))$ — remains open in general. For the even-cycle case, Grósz, Methuku, and Tompkins (2020) sharpened the Kühn-Osthus bounds on $C_4$-free subgraphs of $C_{2k}$-free graphs. The related stronger conjecture of Verstraëte discussed in the OPG has been partially disproved by Conlon, Mulrenin, and Pohoata (2026), who showed that $C_{10}$-free graphs need not contain $C_8$-free subgraphs with a constant fraction of edges.

since_posted.title: On subgraphs of $C_{2k}$-free graphs and a problem of Kühn and Osthus
since_posted.authors: Dániel Grósz, Abhishek Methuku, Casey Tompkins
since_posted.claim: Proves the sharp constant $c = 3/8$ for the $C_6$-free case: every $C_6$-free graph has a bipartite $C_4$-free subgraph with at least $3/8$ of its edges, and gives a new short proof of the general Kühn-Osthus $1/(k-1)$ bound for $C_{2k}$-free bipartite graphs, advancing the even-cycle special case of the conjecture.

since_posted.title: Two counterexamples to a conjecture about even cycles
since_posted.authors: David Conlon, Eion Mulrenin, Cosmin Pohoata
since_posted.claim: Disproves Verstraëte's stronger conjecture (explicitly discussed in the OPG) for $\ell=4$, $k=5$: constructs $C_{10}$-free bipartite graphs in which every subgraph containing a constant fraction of edges must contain a $C_8$, using a dense $C_{10}$-free hypercube subgraph and Wenger's construction.

notes: The Keevash-Sudakov-Verstraete paper (arXiv:1107.4715, July 2011, Combinatorica 2013) proves the Erdős-Simonovits conjecture for families {C_{2ℓ}} combined with odd cycles for ℓ∈{2,3,5}, but the arXiv preprint predates the OPG posting (March 2013) so it was excluded from since_posted. The withdrawn preprint arXiv:2501.13036 (Conlon-Mulrenin-Pohoata, January 2025) was a precursor to 2603.24515 and was superseded. The counterexample to Verstraëte's conjecture (2603.24515) does not directly refute the main Erdős-Simonovits compactness conjecture, which remains open for all finite families.
